text: Weil's attention philosophy has found unexpected resonance in contemporary cognitive science. Researchers studying 'default mode network' activity have found that the brain's wandering, self-referential state — the very state Weil called imaginary attention — is its default, its resting position. True presence requires active suppression of this network by the task-positive network. What Weil described as self-evacuation maps closely onto what neuroscientists call 'deactivation of the DMN': the quieting of the brain's inner narrator. The parallel suggests that Weil's spiritual phenomenology anticipated by decades what neuroscience is only now learning to measure.
question: What does the passage identify as the brain's default resting state, corresponding to Weil's concept of imaginary attention?

options.text: Default mode network activity — wandering, self-referential thought
